verdict_reasoning: ATP5F1B encodes the beta subunit of mitochondrial ATP synthase complex V (F1 catalytic core). The protein is a soluble component of the mitochondrial matrix-facing F1 complex, anchored to the inner mitochondrial membrane via its association with the membrane-spanning Fo component. The protein localizes to the mitochondrial matrix and inner membrane interior, with no documented TM domain, GPI anchor, outer-leaflet lipidation, or extracellular surface exposure. HGNC gene-group membership confirms classification in the mitochondrial ATP synthase family, not surface-protein families. No documented cancer ecto-form, cell-death-induced externalization, tissue-restricted surface display, or other contextual surface pool has been reported. This is a canonical mitochondrial-internal protein with no pathway to the outer plasma membrane.
